skill.name: triage
skill.description: Guides issue tracker triage through role-based states for bugs, enhancements, maintainer review, and agent-ready work.
skill.summary: Organize issue triage, clarify reports, and prepare durable briefs for delegated engineering work.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many command-execution and weak-crypto patterns, but review found these are false positives from Markdown code fences, issue labels, and prose examples. The real risk is operational: the skill directs agents to read issue tracker data, post comments, change labels, close issues, run reproduction commands, and write `.out-of-scope/` files, so users should review actions before execution.

medium_findings.title: Issue Tracker Modification Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to query issue tracker content, apply roles, post comments, and close issues. These actions are legitimate for triage, but they can affect public project state if performed without maintainer confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly requires issue tracker reads and writes. The intent is maintainer triage, but the operations can change labels, comments, and issue status.

medium_findings.title: Repository Knowledge Base Writes
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to create, update, or delete `.out-of-scope/` Markdown files when feature requests are rejected or reconsidered. This is expected behavior, but it modifies repository files and should be reviewed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file modification instructions are direct and semantically tied to the skill purpose. No destructive or hidden behavior was found, but repository writes are a meaningful operational risk.

low_findings.title: Command Execution Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many Ruby or shell backtick executions, but the reviewed files are Markdown instructions and examples. Backticks mark labels, file names, templates, and command examples rather than executable code in the skill package.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged evidence appears inside Markdown prose or fenced examples. No script file, runtime hook, or automatic command launcher was found in the scanned files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but review found no cryptographic implementation or hashing logic. The alerts appear to be triggered by ordinary words, headings, examples, or Markdown file extensions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no encryption, hashing, or credential-handling code. The cited lines are triage documentation, not executable security-sensitive logic.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Use During Bug Reproduction
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill permits agents to run tests or commands while reproducing bugs. This is common engineering practice, but commands should be inspected before execution when issue text supplies steps.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command-running instruction is explicit. Risk depends on the repository and reporter-provided reproduction steps, so it is a controlled but real concern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Issue Tracker Write Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes posting comments, changing roles, and closing issues. These operations are not malicious, but they can produce unwanted public changes if not confirmed by a maintainer.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instructions clearly include issue tracker mutation. The workflow also asks for maintainer direction, which lowers severity.

content.value_statement: Maintainers lose time sorting unclear reports and repeated feature requests. This skill structures issue triage, records decisions, and prepares agent-ready briefs.
actual_capabilities: Classifies issues as bugs or enhancements using defined triage roles.
Guides movement between needs-triage, needs-info, ready-for-agent, ready-for-human, and wontfix states.
Builds durable agent briefs with current behavior, desired behavior, interfaces, acceptance criteria, and scope boundaries.
Surfaces prior rejected feature requests from `.out-of-scope/` records during triage.
Provides templates for needs-info comments and out-of-scope decision records.
Requires AI-generated issue comments to include a disclosure disclaimer.
limitations: Requires access to an issue tracker and the project repository to complete the full workflow.
Does not automatically decide unusual state transitions without maintainer confirmation.
Does not replace maintainer judgment for product scope, design decisions, or manual verification.
May need another skill or manual process to gather detailed reporter information.

use_cases.title: Review incoming issue queues
use_cases.description: Summarize unlabeled, needs-triage, and updated needs-info issues so maintainers can choose what needs attention first.

use_cases.title: Prepare work for coding agents
use_cases.description: Turn a confirmed bug or enhancement into a durable brief with acceptance criteria and boundaries.

use_cases.title: Track rejected feature requests
use_cases.description: Record out-of-scope decisions and reuse them when similar requests appear later.

prompt_templates.title: Show Issues Needing Attention
prompt_templates.prompt: Use /triage to show issues that need attention. Group them by unlabeled, needs-triage, and needs-info with new reporter activity.

prompt_templates.title: Triage One Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Use /triage on issue #42. Read the issue, comments, labels, prior triage notes, and out-of-scope records. Recommend a category and state.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare an Agent Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Use /triage to move issue #42 to ready-for-agent. Draft an agent brief with behavior, key interfaces, acceptance criteria, and out-of-scope items.

prompt_templates.title: Handle Repeated Rejections
prompt_templates.prompt: Use /triage to compare this enhancement request with `.out-of-scope/` records. If it matches a prior rejection, draft the maintainer confirmation and update plan.

output_examples.input: Show me anything that needs my attention.
output_examples.output: A grouped issue summary with counts for unlabeled, needs-triage, and updated needs-info issues, ordered oldest first.
A confirmation of planned role changes and a draft agent brief containing the problem, desired behavior, acceptance criteria, and scope limits.

output_examples.input: This feature request looks out of scope.
output_examples.output: A draft decision record for `.out-of-scope/`, a maintainer-facing issue comment, and the proposed wontfix closure action.

best_practices: Confirm label changes, public comments, and issue closures before applying them.
Use concrete acceptance criteria when preparing work for an agent.
Check prior triage notes and out-of-scope records before asking reporters for more information.
anti_patterns: Do not post issue comments without the required AI disclosure disclaimer.
Do not move conflicting state labels without asking the maintainer first.
Do not create vague agent briefs that reference fragile file paths or line numbers.

faq.question: What issue states does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses needs-triage, needs-info, ready-for-agent, ready-for-human, and wontfix states.

faq.question: Can it triage both bugs and feature requests?
faq.answer: Yes. It classifies issues as bug or enhancement, then recommends the correct workflow state.

faq.question: Does it automatically close issues?
faq.answer: It can guide closure for wontfix issues, but maintainer confirmation should happen before public changes.

faq.question: What is an agent brief?
faq.answer: An agent brief is a durable issue comment that defines behavior, interfaces, acceptance criteria, and scope for delegated work.

faq.question: Why does it use `.out-of-scope/` files?
faq.answer: Those files preserve rejected feature decisions and help identify duplicate requests later.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
